assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, the Ecosystem Detection section explicitly states: 'Library: openssl-libs (system RPM package, not a Cargo/npm source dependency)' and 'Ecosystem: **RPM**'. The ecosystem is identified as RPM based on the library name openssl-libs being a system RPM package, with the lock file identified as rpms.lock.yaml. Cargo is explicitly excluded.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Version Impact table includes all five versions from the 2.2.x stream: 2.2.0 (3.0.7-25.el9_3, YES), 2.2.1 (3.0.7-27.el9_4, YES), 2.2.2 (3.0.7-27.el9_4, YES), 2.2.3 (3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO), and 2.2.4 (3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO). The Dependency Chain section references rpms.lock.yaml: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present --> explicit install' and 'Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs pinned in rpms.lock.yaml)'. The affects-versions.md also confirms 'Based on rpms.lock.yaml analysis at pinned commits from security-matrix.md.'

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context classifies the package origin as explicit install because openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml, not inherited from the base image (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Dependency Chain (Step 2.3.5) section explicitly states: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present --> explicit install' and 'Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs pinned in rpms.lock.yaml)'. The classification is 'explicit install' (not base image), and the reasoning is that openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, line 3 states: 'Ecosystem: RPM (system package, explicit install) -- single task for Konflux release repo.' Only one jira.create_issue call is shown. In data-extraction.md, line 35 confirms: 'Remediation path: single task (Konflux release repo only, no upstream step)'. The task targets rhtpa-release.0.4.z (the Konflux release repo). There is no second upstream backport task.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, the task description contains all required sections: '## Repository' (rhtpa-release.0.4.z), '## Target Branch' (main), '## Description' (Remediate CVE-2026-40215...), '## Implementation Notes' (update openssl-libs package version...), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (two checklist items). There is no '## Files to Modify' section, which is correct per remediation-templates.md line 186: 'Files to Modify and Files to Create are intentionally omitted.' The task-description-template.md rules (line 57) allow omitting sections that don't apply.
